meta.title: The State of Returns on Solana

transcript: Once in a generation, the center of gravity in financial markets move. Quietly, almost without anyone noticing, it moved to Solana. In financial markets, returns are the truth. And the truth is that Solana is now producing more real, verifiable economic returns than any other chain in crypto. My name is Ben Nadareski, I'm the co-founder and CEO of Solstice, the largest Solana-born and Solana-native stablecoin in the world offering one-click access to institutional grade yields. Solstice, along with all the incredible companies here today, are evolving at light speed in delivering the next generation of internet capital markets. And today I'm here to showcase the state of returns across the Solana ecosystem. We can look at the state of returns in Solana in a three-stage evolution. From 2020 to 2022, returns on Solana were driven by subsidies, not systems. Users were chasing token emissions, not market structure. And returns were externally funded, not earned. Capital was shallow but hyper-mobile, responding instantly to incentive changes, resulting in massive TVL spikes. Users bridged capital from other L1s into Solana to chase APY differentials. Yield behavior showed rotational opportunism rather than chain loyalty. From 2022 to 2025, Solana purged the free money psychology and re-centered on durability. Capital began rotating towards protocols with real revenue, audited risk frameworks, and transparency. Liquid staking became the first credible chain-wide passive yield source with 7 to 8% base yield plus an MEV uplift. Jito introduced MEV sharing, pushing LST yields into the 8 to 10% zone, with transparency. mSOL, JitoSOL, bSOL and later LST derivatives became the foundation for loops, structured products, and synthetics. Yield vaults evolved from farming into structured strategies. Kamino pioneered risk-rated disclosure-oriented vaults that removed guesswork from leveraged loops. Yield became programmable, audited, repeatable. Stablecoin depth turned into the true cost of capital advantage, deepening Curve-like dynamics into tighter spreads, lower vol, and cheaper leverage. Institutional capital shifted from observation to allocation. And this was the period where the industry began recognizing Solana as a return machine. Users no longer looked for yield in protocols. They looked for yield through Solana's return architecture. Now bringing us to the present in 2025, Solana's ecosystem is defining institutional-scale returns. Let's look at some of the key figures driving these returns. We saw over $4 billion in spot DEX volumes per day with over $320 billion in Q3 2025 from great partners like Raydium, Orca, Meteora, and others supporting real spread capture, arbitrage, and routing revenue. Solana's liquid staking layer surpassed $10.7 billion with teams like Helius, Jito, Marinade, Sanctum, and others offering base yield plus MEV becoming the risk-free rate on Solana. There are over $1.7 billion in perps open interest with teams like Drift, Zeta, Cypher, and others offering stable funding markets and real basis trades. Perps and funding markets are now core yield engines on Solana. There are over 13, 14 billion dollars in Solana... in stablecoin volumes on Solana with Solstice, USDG, Sky, Cash, Anchorage, and others driving the cost of capital down. Stablecoin yield opportunities offer returns such as structured stable products that combine funding rates, LST backing, and perps hedges to deliver stable target APYs. And lower volatility of stablecoin pools show more predictable returns. RWAs are beginning the next wave of institutional activity on Solana with over $1 billion in on-chain RWAs across multi-asset class exposure, highlighting recent partnerships with R3 accessing tens of billions in RWA pathways over to Solana. At Solstice, we're doing our part. Since September 30th, we have grown to over 330 million in TVL into a Solana-native stablecoin with one-click access to 15% yields over the last 12 months. This is an example of a composable yield-bearing asset that partners across the DeFi stack as many companies are today to enable a full DeFi unlock of returns on Solana. Taking a deeper look at the current return layer available on Solana, native SOL staking yields are at 7.1 to 7.6 APY baseline pre-MEV. LST yields at 8.5 to 10.2% APY including MEV uplift. And MEV contribution to validator yields now at 120 to 180 bips annually on top of staking rewards for high-efficiency operators. Next up in this stack is composability, liquidity efficiency, and routing-driven returns. Jupiter routing efficiencies improved LP realized APY by 150 to 300 bips annually from reduced slippage and optimized pathways. Atomic arbitrage opportunities deliver 5 to 20 bips per trade, compounding for bots and vaults into double-digit APYs. Liquidity providers returns on concentrated AMMs are typically 12 to 30% APY on SOL pairs depending on volatility, points incentives included. Solana blockspace cost stability offers near zero fee... fees pressure with greater than 95% of strategy gross yield, unlike EVM chains where gas erodes returns materially. Next up in this layer is perps, funding markets, basis trades, and composing the derivatives yield layer. Average funding rates on SOL perps oscillates in the positive 8% to negative 6% annualized band, allowing real carry opportunities on both sides. Delta-neutral SOL basis trades annualized returns of 10 to 18% during stable funding periods with low volatility. Bitcoin and ETH perps on Solana offering 2 to 8% annualized funding capture. Cross-venue funding arbitrage, CeFi to Solana, historically yielding 8 to 15% annualized risk-managed with hedges. Market maker spreads plus rebate returns offering 20 to 120 bips per day on high volume pairs. Layer 4, as was just discussed, is the stablecoin yield, synthetic dollars, and cash equivalent returns. Stablecoin yield carry offers 7 to 12% APY with hedged... hedged with perps short. Unlevered stable vaults offer 5 to 7% APYs driven by LST backing and liquidity fees. USDC, USDT lending rates on Solana are at 3 to 6% APY stables with Solend, Kamino, and others as key examples due to the reduced borrow friction. And finally, on the top layer, institutional strategy performance. A performance risk-managed yield layer. Institutional LST plus hedge strategies produce 8 to 14% APYs with volatility compared to TradFi short duration credit. Composite Solana return stack portfolio, which is the LST plus MEV plus stable carry plus perps hedge trade, has historically offered 12 to 20% APY with low vol uncorrelated to SOL perps. All the revolutionary teams here today have collectively created this Solana Yield Superstructure such that institutional capital and retail capital can participate simultaneously. This is battle-tested and actively scaling to billions. We are here today because of Solana, and together these return strategies are hyper-scaling at light speed. The call to action is to scale into these new products and services with continued investment and active deployment across the Solana's DeFi stack. And with a little bit of alpha, one of those investments including Solstice's own presale announcing on the winter solstice this year in December. Solana is positioned for large-scale net new capital from institutions, funds, retail investors, all experiencing crypto for the first time to deploy into the ecosystem with sticky capital. This is what internet capital markets is built for. The most exciting evolution in finance is not happening on Wall Street. It's happening right here on Solana. Thank you.

tags.impact_reason: The keynote by Solstice CEO highlights the evolution and current state of returns on Solana, emphasizing institutional-scale returns and significant figures in DEX volumes and liquid staking, meriting a score of 7.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a massive, futuristic pipeline as the Semantic Anchor because the headline and transcript repeatedly use terms like 'pipe,' 'pipeline,' and 'bridge' to describe the flow of tens of billions in Real World Assets (RWAs) to Solana. This visual metaphor directly represents the 'Solana Yield Superstructure' being built. I included the diegetic text '$10B+' as a holographic flow-rate monitor on the pipeline to emphasize the immense scale of the R3 partnership mentioned in the story.
visual_idea.description: A vast, dark space dominated by a massive, translucent pipeline that recedes into the background, representing the Solana Yield Superstructure. The pipeline, made of polished chrome and shimmering glass, carries glowing streams of data symbolizing RWAs. In the foreground, a crisp, holographic display projects '$10B+' from the side of the pipe, acting as a diegetic status monitor. The background has immense depth, with other glowing architectural data structures faintly visible, suggesting a sprawling digital city.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A vast, dark space is dominated by a massive, translucent pipeline that recedes into the profound background, representing the Solana Yield Superstructure. The colossal pipeline, a magnificent fusion of highly polished chrome and shimmering, translucent glass, meticulously designed as a conduit for light, allows glowing streams of data, symbolizing RWAs, to visibly flow within its immense structure. The chrome surfaces exhibit sharp, reflective gleams and intricate architectural details. In the immediate foreground, a crisp, holographic display projects luminous '$10B+' text, shimmering with internal light, directly from the side of the pipe, acting as a diegetic status monitor embedded within the digital realm. The background has immense depth, with other faintly visible, glowing architectural data structures receding into the distance, forming a sprawling, intricate digital city, reminiscent of a colossal data cathedral, imbued with architectural precision and atmospheric depth. The entire scene is dramatically illuminated by intricate volumetric lighting, with ethereal light rays cutting through a subtle atmospheric haze, enhancing the sense of epic scale and depth, creating cinematic lighting with sharp contrasts. Composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, emphasizing monumental height, epic scale, and substantial negative space at the top to convey grandeur. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, high dynamic range, realistic materials.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a massive, futuristic pipeline as the Semantic Anchor because the headline and transcript repeatedly use terms like 'pipe,' 'pipeline,' and 'bridge' to describe the flow of tens of billions in Real World Assets (RWAs) to Solana. This visual metaphor directly represents the 'Solana Yield Superstructure' being built. I included the diegetic text '$10B+' as a holographic flow-rate monitor on the pipeline to emphasize the immense scale of the R3 partnership mentioned in the story.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot focusing on a complex junction of the main RWA pipeline. Several smaller, glowing fiber-optic cables snake into a polished chrome manifold, merging their light into a single, larger translucent conduit. The focus is on the intricate engineering, the interplay of light reflecting off the metallic surfaces, and the swirling energy of the combined data streams within the pipe. The lighting is dramatic and high-contrast.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A tight, centrally focused macro shot revealing a complex junction of the main RWA pipeline. The frame is filled with intricate engineering details. Several smaller, intensely glowing fiber-optic cables snake into a highly polished chrome manifold, reflecting warm amber light and casting sharp specular highlights. These cables meticulously merge their light into a single, larger translucent conduit, through which the swirling energy of combined data streams is vividly visible, exhibiting subtle subsurface scattering effects. The lighting is dramatically high-contrast, featuring intense volumetric light beams that highlight the metallic surfaces and the glowing conduits, creating deep, atmospheric shadows and strong falloff outside the illuminated areas. Emphasize architectural precision and futuristic grandeur, akin to a 'Data Cathedral' aesthetic. The composition is symmetrical, with the junction as the central focal point, filling the 1:1 square frame with rich textures and glowing details. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, high dynamic range, cinematic lighting, dramatic chiaroscuro, chrome texture, glass texture, light scattering, chromatic aberration.

editorial.headline: Solstice Reveals R3 Partnership to Pipe Billions in RWAs to Solana
editorial.standfirst: CEO Ben Nadareski outlines the transition to a mature 'Solana Yield Superstructure,' confirming over $1 billion in on-chain RWAs. The stablecoin issuer announced a strategic pipeline with enterprise firm R3 to bridge tens of billions in traditional assets to the network.
key_takeaways: Strategic RWA Pipeline: Solstice is partnering with R3 to open pathways for tens of billions in traditional assets to move on-chain, expanding the current $1B RWA sector.
Yield Stack Maturity: Institutional strategies now offer tiered returns, from 8.5–10.2% for Liquid Staking Tokens (LSTs) to 10–18% for delta-neutral basis trades.
Solstice Presale: The protocol, currently securing $330M TVL with 15% historical yields, will open its token presale on December 21, 2025.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The era of subsidy-driven returns on Solana is officially dead. In its place, a sophisticated "Yield Superstructure" has emerged, defined by audited risk frameworks and deep liquidity rather than inflationary token emissions. **Solstice** CEO Ben Nadareski characterized this shift as a move from "rotational opportunism" to "internet capital markets," where yield is a function of market structure—specifically MEV capture, basis trading, and real-world asset (RWA) integration. For institutional allocators, the alpha lies in the maturation of these layers: Liquid Staking Tokens (LSTs) like **JitoSOL** and **mSOL** have established a risk-free rate of 8.5% to 10.2% (including MEV), providing a hard floor for the entire ecosystem.

Moving up the risk curve, the derivatives layer has become the primary engine for yield enhancement. With over $1.7 billion in open interest across platforms like **Drift**, **Zeta**, and **Cypher**, funding rates have stabilized to allow scalable delta-neutral strategies. Nadareski highlighted that basis trades are currently annualizing between 10% and 18% during stable periods, while cross-venue arbitrage (CeFi to DeFi) offers 8% to 15%. This structural depth allows institutions to execute strategies comparable to TradFi short-duration credit but with superior liquidity profiles. The compression of stablecoin spreads, driven by deep pools on **Curve**-like AMMs, has effectively lowered the cost of capital, making Solana a viable venue for complex structured products.

The most significant forward-looking development is the integration of institutional-grade RWAs. While the chain already hosts over $1 billion in on-chain assets, **Solstice** announced a major partnership with enterprise blockchain firm **R3**. This collaboration aims to unlock pathways for "tens of billions" in traditional assets to migrate to Solana, effectively bridging the gap between permissioned banking ledgers and public chain liquidity. **Solstice** itself has grown to $330 million in TVL since late September, offering a one-click access point to these composable yields. With a presale scheduled for the Winter Solstice (December 21), the protocol is positioning itself as the gateway for the next wave of net-new institutional capital entering the ecosystem.
